title: Application Metadata, Transactions, Events, and Orphans · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds file metadata and object reconciliation state machine, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of file metadata and object reconciliation state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign authoritative fields to application database, S3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation.
Persist idempotent intent, reservation, object identity, expected integrity, upload, quarantine, publish, failure, and expiry transitions.
Choose database-first, object-first, or direct-upload ordering and expose each crash window, compensation, and user-visible state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop System Of Record Boundary
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 3751 during a compliance restore drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop System Of Record Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for assign authoritative fields to application database, s3 object state, tags, events, or derived indexes and document reconciliation. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop System Of Record Boundary, keep System Of Record Boundary connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Orphan Repair
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 9654 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to expose object and database crash windows and require idempotent reconciliation; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Detect and safely repair orphan objects, orphan rows, stale upload intents, missing scans, and mismatched versions under bounded listing cost.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

options.text: Keep the plausible Orphan Repair output and hide the violated file metadata and object reconciliation state machine assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Orphan Repair.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Orphan Repair happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Orphan Repair for file metadata and object reconciliation state machine.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Orphan Repair condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
